entries.summary: One who officiates in sacred duties; in Christian usage, a minister or presbyter.
Plural KJV form using the Webster entry for priest and priesthood-related senses.
An assembly; especially a gathered body of people for worship or religious instruction.
Conformity of heart and life to divine law; holiness, justice, and rectitude in theological use.
Just or in accord with divine law; especially used in theology for the godly or upright.
An estate or possession received by descent, or something possessed by divine grant or appointment.
Of that or this.
In that or this place, time, or thing.
In which; in what thing, time, respect, or writing.
To look upon with attention; also used imperatively to call attention or admiration.
Something presented to God in worship, sacrifice, thanksgiving, or atonement; an oblation.
Plural uses can derive from Webster’s offering entry covering sacrificial and devotional offerings.
The scriptural or solemn plural of brother, used chiefly in serious or biblical language.
Breath, life, disposition, soul, immaterial being, or the divine Spirit depending on context.
The immortal rational part of man; also life, inward affection, person, or appetite in Scripture.
An elevated place for sacrifice; in Christian use, also communion table or figurative place of worship.
The middle; often used for being surrounded by or in the thick of something.
A mutual agreement or stipulation; in theology, the covenant of works, redemption, or grace.
Morally pure, free from sin, and also consecrated or set apart for sacred use.
Burned or consumed by fire; in sacrificial use, tied to burnt-offering as an atoning sacrifice.
Evil in principle or practice; sinful, immoral, pernicious, or opposed to divine law.
To experience lively joy or gladness; also to make joyful or gladden.
For that reason; consequently; or sometimes in return for that.
No direct Webster preview page was found in this sourcing pass; review manually before import.
